name: Roast Boar and Black Bean Chili
recipeIngredient: 4 lb Saddle of wild boar (OR loin-pork with bone in)
1 Jalapeno pepper, seeded and deveined, minced
1 cup Cooked, smoked ham diced (1 cup = 5 oz)
1 tbsp Chopped fresh oregano (or half that if dried)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium bowl, combine the bacon drippings with the crushed garlic, 2 tablespoons of the chili powder, cumin, and freshly ground pepper. Spread over the wild boar and let stand while preparing the beans.
2. Cover the beans with cold water in a large pot. Heat to boiling and boil for two minutes. Turn off the heat and let stand one hour. Drain. Wipe out the pot and return the beans to it, covering with cold water. Heat to boiling. Reduce heat and simmer for 30 minutes. Drain.
3. Preheat the oven to 325°F. Cook the salt pork in boiling water for five minutes. Drain and pat dry. Heat olive oil in a heavy, deep casserole. Stir in the salt pork and cook over medium heat until golden, about 3 minutes.
4. Stir in the onion, minced garlic, and Jalapeno pepper. Cook for one minute. Stir in the ham and cook two more minutes. Add the remaining chili powder to the onion mixture. Stir in the beans, broth, bay leaf, oregano, vinegar, and rum. Mix well.
5. Place the saddle of boar on top of the beans, cover, and place in the middle of the oven. Cook for 1½ to 2 hours or until an internal meat thermometer reads 170°F. Turn the meat twice and stir the beans. Add more broth if dry.
6. Remove the meat and allow it to stand, covered, for 10 to 15 minutes. Meanwhile, skim the fat from the chili. Cut the meat from the bone and into thin slices. Layer over the beans. If desired, stew, covered, to tenderize the meat. Serve with hot rice and a sprinkling of scallions and sieved eggs.
7. Serves 6 generously.
